name: Patch Grid
explanation: A patch-matrix modular voice in the EMS Synthi 100 lineage. The Synthi's pin matrix let anything modulate anything, and its most iconic sound is ring modulation between a played oscillator and a free-running one, giving clangorous, inharmonic, bell-and-drone timbres. Here osc1 tracks the note, Osc2 Freq sets a free-running second oscillator at an absolute pitch (so the ring product goes inharmonic), Ring sets the ring-mod amount, Noise adds the Synthi's coloured noise, all through a resonant low-pass with a decay envelope. Mono, last-note priority. Controls: Osc2 Freq, Ring, Noise, Cutoff, Decay and Level.
